doom.driver: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure.
doom.summary: Trump's 8 p.m. ET 'living in Hell' deadline has arrived and Iran's response was essentially 'no thank you, also we're still shooting missiles.' The IMF chief is now warning 'all roads lead to higher prices and slower growth,' which is the polite economist way of saying civilization is getting expensive. Meanwhile Iran is threatening OpenAI's Abu Dhabi data center, making this the first war where a combatant has specifically targeted a tech company's GPU cluster.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Active US-Israel war against Iran, missed ceasefire deadline, ongoing missile exchanges, and shooting near Israeli consulate in Istanbul all signal multi-front escalation with no off-ramp in sight.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: JD Vance rallying for Orbán in Hungary while RFK Jr. consolidates CDC vaccine panel authority represents simultaneous democratic backsliding across two continents.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: The New Yorker's 'can Sam Altman be trusted' cover story and OpenAI insiders expressing distrust of their own CEO suggests alignment concerns are becoming mainstream, not just academic.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: France repatriating its last gold reserves from the US is a quiet but significant signal of eroding institutional trust in American financial stewardship during wartime.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Iran threatening OpenAI's Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi introduces a novel threat vector where AI infrastructure becomes a military target in conventional interstate conflict.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, ground-level view looking straight up from the rubble-strewn floor of a collapsed Tehran concrete building, the shattered skeleton of the structure framing a narrow corridor of sky above — exposed rebar curling outward like blackened fingers against a hard dark crimson sky, interior walls split open revealing fragments of patterned wallpaper and cracked bathroom tile on the cross-sectioned floors above, a lone civilian figure silhouetted small and upright amid the debris far below in the lower quarter of the frame, scale dwarfed by the collapsed multi-story facade looming on either side, a massive state billboard partially visible high on one crumbling wall showing bold Farsi calligraphy and a graphic of narrow blue-green water representing the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian tricolor flags — green white red with central emblem — hanging torn and suspended mid-air from broken balcony railings, Soviet-influenced cooling tower silhouettes visible through the gap in the destroyed skyline against the bleached crimson atmosphere, charcoal smoke columns rising vertically into the oppressive flat sky, firelight reflections catching the bent rebar and shattered glass still clinging to window frames, everything bathed in dark crimson and charcoal tones, high contrast crushing shadows, no antialiasing, no text.

dawn.driver: Artemis II astronauts swung by the moon, broke an Apollo record, and saw an eclipse. The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
dawn.blurb: HUMANS BREAK APOLLO RECORD, ECLIPSE FROM THE MOON
dawn.summary: While the world watches deadline clocks tick down over the Strait of Hormuz, four human beings broke a 54-year-old Apollo distance record while watching a solar eclipse from lunar orbit — a reminder that our species still reaches outward even when it's tearing itself apart down here. The Artemis II crew completed every key milestone and is now heading home Friday, carrying lunar science that will inform base-building and international cooperation on the Moon. In the shadow of war, humanity quietly set a new high-water mark for how far we can go.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Artemis II broke the Apollo-era distance record and gathered new lunar science data during its flyby, advancing humanity's roadmap for sustained Moon exploration.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Four astronauts witnessed a solar eclipse from lunar orbit — an experience no human had seen in over half a century, described as 'truly hard to describe' and 'amazing.'

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Taiwan's opposition leader traveled to China calling herself a 'bridge for peace,' a rare act of cross-strait diplomacy ahead of a critical U.S.-China summit.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Used EV sales are spiking alongside gas prices, suggesting market forces — even painful ones — may accelerate the shift away from fossil fuels faster than policy alone could.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: India and China held their first bilateral EV and renewable energy talks in five years, with a delegation visiting Beijing to seek cooperation on batteries and clean tech amid the oil shock.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Interior of the Orion capsule looking outward through a wide curved window, the cramped cockpit environment rendered in warm amber and rich gold instrument glow — panel lights, screen numerals, indicator arrays casting honeyed light across every surface in deep emerald shadow. The window dominates the composition, framing an enormous Moon filling nearly the entire view outside, its ancient grey-white cratered surface rendered in extraordinary geological detail, ridges and maria glowing under harsh sunlight with zero atmospheric haze, hard star-points burning white against absolute black space. Earth hangs visible at the window's edge, backlit and partially eclipsed, a perfect ring of saturated golden-white solar corona blazing around its darkened silhouette — warm fire-gold light bleeding into the cold void. Four astronaut figures float weightless in the foreground, pressed against the capsule glass, faces catching cool lunar reflected light from below and warm amber instrument glow from behind, one hand braced on the window frame, another pointing outward, hair drifting loose in zero gravity. A glowing green numerical display on the instrument panel shows a mission distance counter. The entire scene is color-graded in rich gold and vibrant emerald, saturated and alive, afternoon-warmth tones flooding the capsule interior while cold space burns beyond the glass.
